reason: The candidate correctly states exactly-once delivery is impossible in distributed systems and explains why (can't distinguish slow vs lost ack), matching the Two Generals reasoning. It correctly identifies the standard approach as at-least-once delivery plus idempotent consumers/dedup keys, and even notes this yields effectively-exactly-once processing, matching the reference's core claims. Additional detail (transactional outbox, Kafka EOS caveats) is accurate and doesn't contradict the reference.
